practicals.title: Practical 1: Accept and display hexadecimal numbers
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to accept five 64-bit hexadecimal numbers from the user, store them in an array, and display the accepted numbers.

practicals.title: Practical 2: String length calculation
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to accept a string and display its length.

practicals.title: Practical 3: Count positive and negative numbers
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to count the number of positive and negative numbers from an array.

practicals.title: Practical 4: Non-overlapped block transfer
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to perform non-overlapped block transfer without using string specific instructions. The data block can be defined in the data segment.

practicals.title: Practical 5: Overlapped block transfer
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to perform overlapped block transfer using string specific instructions. The data block can be defined in the data segment.

practicals.title: Practical 6: Multiplication using successive addition
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to perform multiplication of two 8-bit hexadecimal numbers using the successive addition method.

practicals.title: Practical 7: Find largest number
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to find the largest among given Byte, Word, Double Word, or 64-bit numbers.

practicals.title: Practical 8: Arithmetic operations using switch case
practicals.desc: Write a switch case driven X86/64 ALP to perform 64-bit hexadecimal arithmetic operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division using suitable macros. Define a procedure for each operation.

practicals.title: Practical 9: Hex to BCD conversion
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to convert a 4-digit hexadecimal number into its equivalent BCD number.

practicals.title: Practical 10: BCD to Hex conversion
practicals.desc: Write an X86/64 ALP to convert a 5-digit BCD number into its equivalent hexadecimal number.

practicals.title: Practical 11: Study of motherboard
practicals.desc: Study the motherboard by understanding its components such as block diagram, processor socket, expansion slots, SATA, RAM, form factor, BIOS, internal connectors, external ports, peripherals, data transfer, display, audio, networking, overclocking, and cooling.
